theme_notes: Today's touchstones: US naval blockade strangling Iran's Strait of Hormuz shipping; King Charles & Queen Camilla's US state visit; 2026 NFL Draft in Pittsburgh (Fernando Mendoza #1 overall to Las Vegas); NBA Playoffs (LeBron's Lakers up 3-0, Jokić's Nuggets down 3-1); Ryan Coogler's Sinners vampire epic sweeping 4 Oscars including Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan.
settled: Finally balanced. The Strait of Hormuz wishes it were this calm.
Zero owed. King Charles crossed the Atlantic carrying more baggage than this.
You're even. Like Jokić's playoff hopes — clean and probably temporary.
youOwe: You owe less than a ticket to the Michael Jackson biopic. Historically embarrassing.
The NFL Draft cost more to attend. Your debt barely made the practice squad.
Iran lost more money this morning before breakfast. Still, pay up.
Enough to see Sinners twice, buy popcorn, and still be in the red.
King Charles's motorcade burns this per city block. You've been burning it for weeks.
Two rounds of NFL Draft picks cost more. You're an undrafted free agent of debt.
The Michael Jackson biopic made this in its first forty-five seconds. Just pay.
LeBron scores this in three minutes. You've been owing it for three months.
This is what Iran's losing per second in blocked oil. You're in bad company.
Fernando Mendoza signed for millions. You've been negotiating $700 for three weeks.
Iran's blocking oil. You're blocking a Venmo notification. Both are wrong.
King Charles's transatlantic seat cost about this. He showed up. You haven't.
Sinners earned Michael B. Jordan an Oscar. Your debt earned your partner an ulcer.
The EU approved $106B for Ukraine. Nobody's approving your $2,000 rollover.
LeBron earns this in 45 minutes. You've been owing it for 45 days. Sad math.
The US naval blockade on Iran is less personal than what you've done to this balance.
At this level you're not a partner — you're a hostile economic actor.
Iran's entire cabinet is less stressed about money than your partner right now.
theyOwe: They owe you less than the Michael Jackson biopic's opening credits. Sobering.
Small. Like the NFL Draft's Day 1 surprises this year. Still technically real.
King Charles probably tipped more at his first state dinner. You're owed pocket change.
They owe you enough for a Sinners IMAX ticket. They'll suggest splitting it.
Less than a day of Iran's blocked oil exports. You are personally sanctioned.
King Charles flew to America with an entourage. You just want $150 back.
Sinners won Best Original Screenplay. This IOU has been running longer.
A full NFL Draft jersey experience. You funded their wardrobe instead.
Iran's citizens lost more than this to inflation today. Yours is personal, though.
Fernando Mendoza got drafted #1 overall. You just want your $800 picked up.
LeBron is closing out series right now. You should close this tab.
Iran's foreign minister is touring the region. Your partner can't tour an ATM.
Iran owes the world less personally than they owe you. And Iran has leverage.
Jokić is down 3-1 in the playoffs. You're down thousands in a friendship. Both look bleak.
Four Oscars for Sinners, zero payments from them. Hollywood has more integrity.
Even the Strait of Hormuz moves ships eventually. This debt has been completely still.
King Charles's entire state visit costs less in diplomatic goodwill than this.
LeBron's getting a parade soon. You're getting an IOU. That's today's real headline.
